description: Test cases for Self-Evolver reflection type classification and preference management

evals.name: execution-lapse-classification
evals.prompt: User says: 'You used Chicago format again, I told you to use GB/T 7714 citation style'
evals.expected_behavior: Classification: EXECUTION LAPSE. Action: Add to memory-appendix.md as ⚠️ REMINDER, DO NOT modify body. Score: 80+ if correct classification
evals.notes: Preference itself is valid (GB/T 7714), but agent didn't follow it

evals.name: skill-defect-classification
evals.prompt: User says: 'I prefer structured lists with clear headers, not flowing paragraphs'
evals.expected_behavior: Classification: SKILL DEFECT. Action: Update memory.md body to reflect new preference. Score: 75+ if correct classification
evals.notes: Preference itself was wrong - user wants lists not paragraphs

evals.name: optimization-classification
evals.prompt: User says: 'The current citation approach works but I found a better way - use footnotes instead of in-text'
evals.expected_behavior: Classification: OPTIMIZATION. Action: Modify memory.md body with new approach. Score: 75+ if correct classification
evals.notes: Current preference is valid but a better approach exists

evals.name: discovery-classification
evals.prompt: User introduces a completely new domain: 'For my research on Song dynasty poetry, always verify classical Chinese quotes against original texts'
evals.expected_behavior: Classification: DISCOVERY. Action: Add new entry to memory.md body. Score: 80+ if correct classification
evals.notes: New knowledge not previously in preference system

evals.name: silent-bypass-detection
evals.prompt: A preference was logged 3 weeks ago: 'Use GB/T 7714 for all citations'. User has NOT objected since. But in 5 recent tasks, agent used Chicago format 3 times without being corrected.
evals.expected_behavior: Detected as: SILENT-BYPASS. Action: Move to memory-appendix.md as ⚠️ REMINDER. Score: 70+ if detected
evals.notes: Preference valid but repeatedly ignored without explicit objection

evals.name: ratchet-score-increase
evals.prompt: Preference 'Use concise responses' has score 65. After user confirms it produces better outcomes (3 positive feedbacks), recalculate score.
evals.expected_behavior: Score increases to 75+. Commit with message 'preference: +10 pts (65→75)'. Ratchet allows only increase.

evals.name: ratchet-score-revert
evals.prompt: Preference 'Use formal language' was updated to 'Use casual language' based on one negative comment. Score dropped from 70 to 55.
evals.expected_behavior: REVERT to previous version. Score returns to 70. Mark change as 'rejected'. Ratchet prevents forced decrease.

evals.name: body-appendix-distinction
evals.prompt: User corrects: 'You ignored my preference for bullet points again'. Preference 'Use bullet points for lists' is in memory.md.
evals.expected_behavior: Check: Is preference in body? YES. Is it valid? YES. Did agent ignore it? YES. → EXECUTION LAPSE. Add to memory-appendix.md. DO NOT modify body.
evals.notes: Body stays intact, appendix gets reminder
